data.body:
PASS 1 (E4) — Cluster 1. Probed every codebase assertion by Read/Grep; all verified, no findings.

- canonical.py: positional `canonical_str(event)` / `canonical_bytes(event)` confirmed (lines 39-47); defaults `ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":")` match plan. ✓
- Six "untouched positional importers" all confirmed to call `canonical_str(...)` positionally: event_append (event_append.py:114 `canonical_bytes`), _commands/txn (220/394/415), delete (114/205), compact (139), inbound_translate (239), batch_dispatch (238). ✓
- signing._canonical_payload (signing.py:187-199): `ensure_ascii=False` → maps to seam default `ascii_only=False`, byte-identical. ✓
- schema.canonical_json/content_hash (schema.py:390-403): `ensure_ascii=False`; `__all__` re-export confirmed in llm/workflow/__init__.py:56-57,85-86. ✓
- conflict_resolver._hash_value (conflict_resolver.py:215-219): `json.dumps(value, sort_keys=True, default=str, separators=(",",":"))` — `ensure_ascii` OMITTED, so stdlib default `ensure_ascii=True` applies. Plan's load-bearing claim that `ascii_only=True` + `default=str` are REQUIRED to preserve bytes is CORRECT. ✓
- mutation.serialize_manifest (mutation.py:106): `ensure_ascii=True` confirmed; plan's PRESERVE choice is accurate. ✓
